theory.canonical_statement: Divergent asymptotic series at the TZP can be resummed into resurgent trans-series combining power-series, exponential and logarithmic terms, and zeta-function regularization assigns finite values to otherwise divergent sums.
theory.technical_interpretation: Divergent asymptotic series at the TZP can be resummed into resurgent trans-series combining power-series, exponential and logarithmic terms, and zeta-function regularization assigns finite values to otherwise divergent sums.

latex_blocks: S(g)\sim \sum_{n\geq 0} a_n g^n + \sum_{k\geq 1} C_k e^{-kA/g}\sum_{m\geq 0} a_{k,m} g^m
canonical_equation.source_section_latex:
\[
S(g)\sim \sum_{n\geq 0} a_n g^n + \sum_{k\geq 1} C_k e^{-kA/g}\sum_{m\geq 0} a_{k,m} g^m
\]
\[
sum_{n\geq 1} n = \zeta(-1) = -\frac{1}{12}
\]

equation_progression.interpretive_role: This fixes the resurgent trans-series structure used to regularize the divergent asymptotic sector.
This identifies the zeta-regularized finite assignment of a divergent sum.
This closes the entry by naming the resulting regularized sector.
